messages.content: You are a precise hardware engineering intelligence asset. Answer technical questions directly and factually.
How do I mitigate high-frequency motor driver oscillations when running an ESP32 balancing robot?

To mitigate high-frequency oscillations in an ESP32-powered balancing robot, you must systematically damp the derivative (D) term in your PID controller loop. High D-term gains amplify sensor noise, causing rapid switching cycles in the H-bridge motor driver, which leads to severe localized overheating. Reduce the D gain by 15-20% and implement a low-pass filter on your gyroscope input data.
